_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices indexed to 2015 (base = 100), currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase since 2015. This headline rise reflects an uneven and relatively recent climb, as not all components contributed equally. The basket represents global soft commodity prices, which can be volatile due to market thinness and external shocks. The index offers a snapshot of key components affecting consumer breakfast items internationally.
notes.movers: Coffee prices lead the rise with a 93% increase since 2015, followed by orange juice at +22%, while wheat and sugar increased more modestly at 7% and 5%, respectively. These movements are mainly driven by weather events and crop-specific diseases such as frost and drought in coffee regions and citrus greening and hurricanes impacting orange juice. This reflects the vulnerability of thin soft-commodity markets where supply shocks cause sharp price swings rather than broad inflationary trends.
notes.why: The headline increase is largely driven by a few weather-sensitive crops rather than general market factors. Given the nature of these soft commodities, price inputs can remain elevated for extended periods due to prolonged environmental challenges and limited market liquidity.
